calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And General Mutual Release
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Samantha Sepehr for petitioner Barton Lynch (via Zoom).
Noelani Nasser for objectors William Lynch and Amanda Lynch (via Zoom).
Melanie Emmons, for petitioner Amanda Lynch (via Zoom).
Christopher Jew, for conservatee Peggy Lynch (via zoom).

From the outset of the hearing, it was confirmed that all parties confirmed and approved the settlement agreement, and that Counsel is waving personal notice to Barton Lynch.

The matter is taken under submission with the intent to grant the petition as prayed in the petition and supplement.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And General Mutual Release
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Continued To Apr-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And General Mutual Release
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Noelani Nasser for objectors William Lynch and Amanda Lynch (via zoom).
Christopher Jew for conservatee Peggy Lynch (via zoom).
Ritizi Lam for petitioner Barton Lynch. (via zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the settlement agreement, that parties should sign the agreement in all their capacities, and does the $100,000.00 loan serve the best interest of the conservatee.

Mr. Jew stated that his client doesn't want to hear about to the disputes among the siblings, that talking about the situation makes her upset, and that she has no opinion regarding the trust.

Ms. Nasser stated that the commercial property provides a steady stream of income to the conservatee, and that the siblings only have a single dight interest in the commercial property.

The Court authorizes court appointed counsel, Christopher Jew with expanded powers.

The matter is continued to April 13, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The trustee and conservator to provide a supplemental document, to support their position, one week prior to the hearing.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition Forconfirmation Of Validity Of Estate Plan Documents; Account And Return Of Trust Assets; Double Damages; And Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called together:
1) PCN-24-307711, Conservatorship of Peggy Lynch; and
2) PTR-24-307867, The Lynch Family Revocable Trust.

Appearances:
Samantha Sepehr for petitioner Barton Lynch (via Zoom).
Noelani Nasser for objectors William Lynch and Amanda Lynch (via Zoom).
Melanie Emmons, for petitioner Amanda Lynch (via Zoom).

Regarding the trust matter, counsel reported that case had settled. A Notice of Settlement had been submitted for filing on October 30, 2025, but during the hearing it was noted that the filing had been rejected. Ms. Nasser confirmed that a petition for approval of settlement will be filed shortly, pending signatures. The Court took the petition for Confirmation of Validity of Estate Plan Documents, filed in the trust matter, off calendar.

Regarding the conservatorship matter, the Court noted a supplement is pending in e-filing which had not yet been reviewed. Counsel reported that no objections were anticipated to the petition to transfer trust assests. Discussion was held regarding notice requirements. Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 24, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for review of recent filings.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition Forconfirmation Of Validity Of Estate Plan Documents; Account And Return Of Trust Assets; Double Damages; And Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-04-2025 Continued To Nov-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Forconfirmation Of Validity Of Estate Plan Documents; Account And Return Of Trust Assets; Double Damages; And Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-02-2025 Continued To Nov-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ritzi K. Lam for and with Barton Lynch, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jessica L. Rowe for and with William Lynch and Amanda Lynch, Objectors (via Zoom).

Ms. Rowe stated the proof of service was attached to the objection, that she filed the proof of service last week, and that she will need additional time for formal discovery. The matter is continued to November 4, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

The Court ordered in case PCN-24-307711 the Conservatorship of the Person and Estate that both petitions to be continued to November 4, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

Petition Forconfirmation Of Validity Of Estate Plan Documents; Account And Return Of Trust Assets; Double Damages; And Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jun-30-2025 Continued To Sep-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Forconfirmation Of Validity Of Estate Plan Documents; Account And Return Of Trust Assets; Double Damages; And Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Mar-25-2025 Continued To Jul-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ritzi K. Lam for and with Barton Lynch, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jessica L. Rowe for and with William Lynch and Amanda Lynch, Objectors (via Zoom).

The court and counsel discussed multiple issues raised in the petition. Ms. Lam argued that Dr. Barton Lynch, as a residual beneficiary, is entitled to an accounting under Probate Code Section 15800, and raised concerns about lack of oversight given Amanda Lynch's dual role as conservator and co-trustee. Ms. Rowe argued that the trust instrument limits who is entitled to an accounting and that the co-trustees' actions are monitored through the conservatorship proceedings.

The court took the request for an accounting under submission and indicated that it would issue an order. The remainder of the petition (excluding the already-resolved power of attorney issue) was continued to July 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for possible mediation and discovery.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
